title: Lavender and lime lip balm
subtitle: A simple beeswax balm scented with lavender and lime
excerpt: A four-ingredient lip balm made with beeswax, shea butter, jojoba oil, and a small amount of lavender and lime essential oils. A beginner-level anhydrous balm that fills around 8 to 10 standard lip-balm tubes or a small tin.
sourceNotes: Beeswax to oil ratio for lip balm (firm tube consistency): approximately 25 to 30% beeswax to total formula weight. Shea butter and jojoba oil skin compatibility: standard cosmetic reference data. Essential oil safe usage rate for lip products: Tisserand and Young, Essential Oil Safety (2nd ed., 2014), lip-product category has lower maximum levels than body; lavender at 1% and lime at 0.5% are within documented lip-safe range.

recipe.yieldDescription: 8 to 10 lip-balm tubes or 1 small tin (~30 g total)
recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Beeswax percentage stays at 28% of total formula for a firm lip-tube consistency. Reduce to 18 to 20% for a softer tin-balm consistency.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Allow 1 hour to fully set before capping the tubes.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Store in a cool dark place. Lip balm keeps 12 months. Discard if the balm develops a rancid smell, white spots on the surface (mould), or a waxy bloom that does not match the original texture.

glossaryTerms.definition: A preparation containing no water phase. Lip balm is anhydrous and keeps 12 months without a preservative.
The temperature at which a wax fully liquefies. Beeswax melts at 62 to 65°C; the finished lip balm sets firmly at body temperature and will not melt in a warm pocket.

Apply a small amount to the inside of the forearm and leave for 24 to 48 hours before lip use. Lime essential oil has a known photosensitising risk in its expressed form; use steam-distilled lime essential oil in any leave-on product.

content.text: Lip balm is a water-free preparation: melt the hard wax, add the soft wax and liquid oils, cool slightly, add the essential oils, and pour into tubes before the formula sets. The beeswax content (28% of formula weight) gives the balm its firm tube consistency; reducing it produces a softer balm suitable for a tin. Shea butter adds a cushioning feel; jojoba oil prevents the balm feeling too waxy. For a leave-on lip product, use steam-distilled lime essential oil, not cold-pressed. The cold-pressed form contains bergapten, a photosensitising compound reduced or absent in the steam-distilled version. Lavender at 1% and steam-distilled lime at 0.5% of formula weight are within the lip-safe range. This is an

items.prepNote: 28% of formula; yellow or white pellets; yellow has a stronger honey note
30% of formula; adds a smooth, soft feel
40% of formula; prevents the balm feeling overly waxy

content.text: Stand the lip-balm tubes upright in a small cup or rack before starting. Make sure the bottom plugs are screwed down fully so the balm does not leak out on pouring.
Weigh the beeswax pellets and shea butter into the top of the double boiler. Melt over gently simmering water, stirring occasionally. The beeswax has a higher

than the shea butter and will melt last. Once both are fully liquid, add the jojoba oil and stir to combine. Remove from heat and let the mixture cool to around 60°C. Add both essential oils and stir well to distribute evenly.

Pour the melted balm into the lip-balm tubes in a thin, steady stream. Fill each tube to just below the rim. Work quickly; the mixture sets fast once it drops below 55°C. If the formula begins to solidify before pouring is complete, return it briefly to gentle heat and stir. Allow the tubes to set undisturbed for at least 1 hour before capping. If a slight dip forms on top as the balm cools, top up with a small second pour.
on the inside of the forearm for 24 hours before lip use. Beeswax rarely causes reactions. If a propolis sensitivity is known, substitute a plant-based wax.

Store tubes in a cool dark place. Lip balm keeps 12 months; the lime note fades before the lavender. Discard any tube that develops a rancid smell or surface bloom different from the characteristic smooth, firm beeswax texture.
